title: Content List Generator
summary: A dual-runtime desktop tool for content-list exports, verification hashes, plain-text reports, and email-file copy workflows. The repo is organized around the real app code, operational deploy copies, and feature-level parity testing rather than old planning artifacts.

overview.title: Primary Purpose
overview.body: Generate inventory reports from folders and copy supported email files into a clean destination while preserving relative structure.

overview.title: Current App Split
overview.body: Go is the primary desktop runtime for macOS and Linux. Python remains the portable Windows path and ships with native folder pickers plus optional BLAKE3 support.

overview.title: Source Of Truth
overview.body: Top-level README and TODO are the canonical docs, while this dashboard summarizes project shape, dependencies, limitations, and recent cleanup work.

overview.title: Generated Assets
overview.body: Build outputs and packaged release artifacts are treated as disposable products of scripts, not as tracked source files.

tools.title: Content Scan
tools.body: Walks a folder tree, filters excluded files, writes CSV, optionally writes XLSX, generates a plain-text report, and records the selected hash algorithm per row.

tools.title: Verification Hash Modes
tools.body: Go defaults to BLAKE3. Python defaults to SHA-1 and warns if BLAKE3 is selected without the optional package installed.

tools.title: Email Copy
tools.body: Copies supported mail container formats into a destination tree, preserves relative paths, and writes a manifest for downstream verification.

tools.title: Desktop UX
tools.body: Both GUI paths now support native folder pickers, current-file progress text, stop controls, and report output alongside CSV generation.

architecture.title: Go Runtime
architecture.body: main.go, core.go, gui_fyne.go, and the scan helper files make up the desktop runtime and shared workflow logic for the Go path.

architecture.title: Python Runtime
architecture.body: python/content_list_generator.py and python/content_list_core.py implement the Windows-focused runtime while matching the Go workflow surface as closely as possible.

architecture.title: Operational Deploy Copies
architecture.body: deploy/windows/ contains the copy-ready launcher and Python script bundle that users can drop into their Desktop and scripts folders.

architecture.title: Testing Layout
architecture.body: testing/content-scan/ and testing/email-copy/ each carry their own fixtures, generator scripts, and runner scripts, with Go and Python test modules pointed at those fixtures.

docs.title: README
docs.body: Authoritative setup, runtime, packaging, and testing guide.

docs.title: TODO
docs.body: Current follow-up items that still reflect real work instead of stale plans.

docs.title: Testing Overview
docs.body: Feature-oriented testing structure and fixture notes.

openIssues.title: Very Large Scan ETA Smoothing
openIssues.body: ETA is now based on bytes instead of file count, but very large final files can still make the tail feel worse than the estimate suggests.

openIssues.title: Release Identity
openIssues.body: Public repository URL, ownership handoff, final license, and attribution terms still need to be finalized.

openIssues.title: Linux Packaging
openIssues.body: Linux release packaging still needs to be produced and validated from a Linux host.

limitations.title: Dual Runtime Maintenance
limitations.body: The project intentionally keeps separate Go and Python GUI implementations, which means parity work has to be tested instead of assumed.

limitations.title: Python BLAKE3 Dependency
limitations.body: Python can use BLAKE3, but that path depends on an extra package and therefore cannot be assumed on every Windows machine.

limitations.title: Portable Delivery
limitations.body: The repo currently favors portable bundles and script-driven release helpers over installer-style desktop distribution.

dependencies.title: Go
dependencies.body: Primary runtime for the macOS and Linux GUI, plus optional Windows executable builds.

dependencies.title: Python 3 + Tkinter
dependencies.body: Required for the Windows portable runtime and local Python workflow.

dependencies.title: customtkinter
dependencies.body: GUI dependency for the Python desktop app.

dependencies.title: blake3
dependencies.body: Optional Python dependency used only when the user selects BLAKE3 hashing.

recentChanges.title: Docs were consolidated
recentChanges.body: Stale install, smoke-test, issue, and planning docs were folded into a single README, a focused TODO, and this static dashboard.

recentChanges.title: Testing became feature-oriented
recentChanges.body: Fixtures moved out of the old mixed testdata layout into content-scan and email-copy test areas with explicit generators and runners.

recentChanges.title: Generated artifacts stopped being source
recentChanges.body: Tracked build outputs, packaged release files, caches, and mockup leftovers were removed or set up to stay ignored.

recentChanges.title: Runtime and deploy copies were preserved
recentChanges.body: The real Go and Python runtimes, Windows deploy bundle, packaging scripts, and active launchers were kept intact and re-documented.
